response.justification: Strong general software engineering credentials with Staff-level experience and prior IDE tooling work at National Instruments. However, there is a significant gap in the core technical requirements: Java expertise is minimal (last substantial work 10+ years ago), Kotlin experience is absent, and there is no demonstrated knowledge of Spring or enterprise frameworks. Recent focus has been TypeScript/Node.js and AI tooling. Could potentially grow into the role with substantial ramp-up on the JVM ecosystem, but the missing depth in required technologies is material.
strengths: Staff-level experience across multiple companies with proven ability to architect and maintain complex systems
Direct experience building IDE components at National Instruments plus recent developer tooling work on MCP frameworks at Acme
Strong software craftsmanship foundation (TDD, CI/CD, clean architecture) with demonstrated ability to mentor and teach others
concerns: Java and Kotlin expertise not demonstrated—resume shows minimal Java experience with last significant work over a decade ago and zero Kotlin background, despite these being explicit requirements
No evidence of Spring ecosystem knowledge or experience with enterprise frameworks (Hibernate, Quarkus, application servers)—last 4+ years focused on TypeScript/Node.js and event-driven platforms
IDE and JVM tooling experience is dated (2014-2015 at National Instruments), with primary work since then shifted away from JVM-focused domains
